content: Families are made up of different members who have relationships with each other. Communities are groups of families that live close to one another and work together. People in families and communities help each other and share the work.
learning_objectives: Understand that families consist of individuals with relationships.
Recognize that communities are composed of multiple families living closely and collaborating.
Acknowledge the mutual support within families and communities.

content: Families enjoy visiting the mela together, which is a place for exploring, encountering new people, and engaging in fun activities, fostering community support and happiness.
learning_objectives: Understand that the mela is a social hub for families to bond and learn from each other.
Identify how the mela helps families support each other through shared experiences.
Recognize the importance of visiting places like the mela for cultural and social engagement.

content: Playing chhupan-chhupai (hide and seek) is a fun outdoor game where one person hides and the others try to find them. Peek-a-boo is a game for young children that involves hiding and then suddenly reappearing, saying 'peek-a-boo!'.
learning_objectives: Understand the concept of chhupan-chhupai and its rules
Recognize the importance of using senses and memory in chhupan-chhupai
Identify the key elements of peek-a-boo and its purpose

content: Many times, my Dadiji sings songs about the rain. She has taught us a few of the songs, so we join in the singing too.
learning_objectives: Understand that rain songs celebrate the life-giving aspect of rain and its role in nurturing the environment.
Recognize how rain songs are a form of cultural expression that brings families together, creating shared experiences and memories.
Identify the rhythmic patterns and rhymes in rain songs that make them easy to remember and sing, especially for children.

topic: Families Come in All Shapes and Sizes
content: Families are made up of parents, children, and sometimes grandparents and other relatives. Families can be big or small, but what matters is the love and care within the family. Sharing food and playing games with family members strengthens bonds.
learning_objectives: Identify different types of families
Describe ways to show love and care to family members
Explain why sharing food with animals is important

content: Families are made up of different members who love and support each other, including animals and nature. Various activities strengthen family bonds.
learning_objectives: Recognize the importance of caring for animals and nature as part of a family.
Identify ways families show love and support to each other.
List activities that families can do together to strengthen their bond.

content: The concept of mutual help and learning within a family and community is essential for personal growth and maintaining strong relationships.
learning_objectives: Mutual help strengthens family bonds and ensures that everyone contributes to the household.
Learning from family and friends helps in acquiring new skills and knowledge.
Examples of mutual help in family include helping parents and grandparents with household chores.

content: Collect some leaves and flowers that have fallen on the ground and create a rangoli. How do you help your family? Who is the oldest person in your family? Who is the youngest person in your family? Who is the tallest person in your family? Do you have any members in your family who do not live with you? How often do you meet them? Note to the Teacher: Explain to children that a rangoli can be made using a variety of materials. Activity 2 can be done on the classroom floor, under supervision. Reprint 2026-27. Family and Friends: Look at the pictures below and write down Bela's relationship with all the members of her family. Core Concept: Explain clearly in simple language. Key Ideas: - Point 1 - Point 2 - Point 3 Deep Understanding: Explain why the concept works with examples. Check Understanding: 1. Question 2. Question 3. Question Practice: 1. Exercise 2. Exercise Common Mistakes: - Mistake - Mistake Quick Revision: - Key point - Key point - Key point
learning_objectives: Identify family members and their roles
Describe ways to help family members
Recognize the importance of family relationships

content: Families are made up of various members with different relationships to each other, each family member contributes to the household in their unique way, extended family members are part of our family network, even if they don't live with us
learning_objectives: Draw a picture of your family members and label the relationships between them
Identify the roles each family member plays in the household
List relatives who are not in your immediate family but are related through your parents
Understand that family work is a shared responsibility
Recognize extended family members as part of the family
Write a short paragraph about your family's roles

content: We joined them and made two teams. Dadaji brought a ball. He hit the stones and scattered them all! We ran to pile the stones as fast as we could. Rohit, Niku and Ronnie tried to hit us with the ball to make us lose. It was fun. We began to feel tired You may add to the list other work that your family members do in your home. Work Cooking food Watering plants Mending things (repairing household items, sewing buttons, etc.) Cleaning the house Buying things from the market Who does it? Reprint 2026-27 Family and Friends 17 after some time, so Dadaji asked us to rest for a while. After about 20 minutes he bought us some ice cream. Soon it began to get dark and we went home. We had a lot of fun playing, laughing and eating together!
learning_objectives: Family members show their love and care for each other by participating in various activities together.
These activities include playing games, cooking, and assisting with household chores.
The time spent together strengthens family bonds and creates lasting memories.

topic: Discuss and Activity in Our Wondrous World | Class 3
content: What do you learn from elders in your family? We care for many others who are not in our family. Who are they? How do we care for them? Find and Circle Circle five words related to games in the grid below. One example is done for you.
learning_objectives: Discuss learning from family elders
Identify individuals cared for outside the family
Find and circle words related to games

content: A mela is a local fair or festival that is an important cultural event providing a platform for people to come together, share experiences, and celebrate their heritage.
learning_objectives: Understand what a mela is
Recognize the importance of melas for communities
Identify activities typically found at a mela

content: Coordination with family and friends is essential for a smooth trip. Planning the mode of transportation is crucial for reaching the destination. Being prepared with essentials ensures comfort during the trip.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of coordination with family and friends before a trip.
Recognize the significance of planning the mode of transportation for a successful trip.
Identify essential items to pack for a trip.

content: The text describes a scene where a family travels to a mela using a bus and an autorickshaw.
learning_objectives: Identify the different modes of transportation mentioned in the text
Explain why a reserved seat for older people is important on public transportation
Describe how using different modes of transportation benefits the family's trip to the mela

content: A mela is a festive gathering with various attractions like games, toys, and food stalls.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of safety and cleanliness at a mela.
Identify different attractions available at a mela.
Recognize how visiting a mela can be educational.

content: The Kumbh Mela is a significant Hindu festival held every twelve years, attracting millions of pilgrims to the sacred rivers of Ganga, Yamuna, Godavari, and Shipra.
learning_objectives: Identify the four sacred rivers where the Kumbh Mela is held
Explain why millions of people gather at the Kumbh Mela
Discuss the significance of ritual bathing during the festival

content: Draw and label different vehicles that you see around your school and home. Organise a mock mela in your classroom. Discuss and plan the different roles, stalls, and games that are part of a mela. Use learning-teaching materials available locally, e.g., toys, puppets, play money, miniatures, and clay models to set up your stalls and role-play buyers and sellers.
learning_objectives: Identify the purpose of a mela
Describe the roles of buyers and sellers
Explain the importance of games and stalls in a mela

content: Festivals are joyous occasions that bring communities together to celebrate cultural heritage, religious beliefs, or seasonal changes.
learning_objectives: Identify common activities during festivals
Explain how festivals preserve cultural traditions
Name a festival celebrated in your community or country

content: The importance of road safety and respecting traffic rules.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of following road safety rules.
Recognize the consequences of not respecting traffic signs.
Appreciate how recognizing local flora and festivals enhances cultural knowledge.

content: In this section, we explore the significance of spring festivals in different cultures, focusing on the celebration of nature's rebirth after winter. These festivals often involve the use of flowers and plants, which are symbols of new life and growth, and are a way to honor and thank nature for its bounty and beauty.
learning_objectives: Understand the significance of spring festivals in different cultures
Identify the role of flowers and plants in spring festivals
Recognize the cultural and spiritual aspects of spring festivals

content: Explore local festivals, their associated foods, attire, and matching exercises.
learning_objectives: Ask elders about seasonal festivals.
List special foods for each festival.
Describe festival attire.
Match foods with festivals.
Explain the concept clearly.
Identify common mistakes.
Revise key points.

content: # Educational Material: Celebrations and Festivals ## Chapter: Celebrations and Festivals ### Section: Concept Block 22 ### Core Concept Festivals are joyous occasions that bring communities together to celebrate cultural or religious events. They often involve special foods, new clothes, and various forms of transportation to visit family and friends.
learning_objectives: Understand that festivals are times for celebrating cultural or religious events.
Identify that special foods are prepared and shared during festivals.
Recognize that festivals often involve traveling to visit relatives and friends.

content: Rishi and his family walked to his Mama's house through a narrow street. The red flowers that bloom in spring are called cherry blossoms.
learning_objectives: Understand the significance of cherry blossoms in Japan
Recognize the use of narrow streets for travel
Identify cherry blossoms as a symbol of spring

content: A cultural practice celebrating the Earth and the Sun through music and dance using traditional instruments like dhol and nagada.
learning_objectives: Identify the main elements of the Sarhul festival.
Explain the symbolic meaning of the instruments used in Sarhul.
Understand the importance of showing gratitude to the Earth and the Sun.

content: Plants are diverse and essential for life; observing plants helps us learn about their importance; plants require specific conditions to thrive
learning_objectives: Understand the diversity and importance of plants in the ecosystem
Recognize the role of plants in providing oxygen, food, and shelter
Identify the essential conditions required for plant growth

content: Plants and animals have a mutual relationship where they support each other. This relationship is based on the observation of how they interact within their ecosystem.
learning_objectives: Understand that plants and animals depend on each other for survival
Recognize the role of animals in seed dispersal
Identify ways plants support animal life

content: Plants exhibit a wide range of physical characteristics.
learning_objectives: Understand that plants come in various sizes, shapes, and textures.
Identify unique features such as leaf texture and fruit color in different plants.
Observe and describe the life cycle of a plant.
Draw and label the different parts of a plant.
Observe and record the growth of a plant over a week.
Avoid confusing plant species based on leaf shape alone.
Recognize the importance of fruit color in plant identification.

content: Trees have a big trunk of wood and many branches with leaves while shrubs are medium-sized plants with several woody stems and branches growing close to the ground; both have roots that absorb water and nutrients; trees and shrubs are essential for providing shade, producing oxygen, and being sources of food and medicine; understanding the differences and importance of trees and shrubs in ecosystems.
learning_objectives: Identify the main differences between trees and shrubs based on their structure and size.
List examples of trees and shrubs found in one's neighborhood.
Explain the importance of trees and shrubs in the environment.
Draw a diagram of a tree and label its main parts.
Write a paragraph on the importance of trees in our lives.

content: Herbs are smaller plants with soft stems that do not become woody, while grasses are a type of herb with thin, flat leaves and hollow stems.
learning_objectives: Identify the differences between herbs and grasses
Understand the importance of herbs and grasses in the ecosystem
Classify plants in a garden as herbs or grasses

content: Climbers and creepers are plants with thin and flexible stems that either climb up other plants or spread across the ground.
learning_objectives: Identify climbers and creepers based on their stem characteristics
Understand how climbers use tendrils to attach and climb
Recognize how creepers spread across the ground

content: Plants that grow by climbing on other plants for support are called climbers. Examples of climbers include Money Plant, Jasmine, Bottle Gourd, Watermelon, and Pumpkin. Plants that grow by creeping along the ground are called creepers.
learning_objectives: Identify climbers and creepers based on their growth patterns
Explain the role of support in the growth of climbers
Differentiate between the growth habits of creepers and climbers

topic: Understanding Plant Life Cycle and Leaf Characteristics
content: Leaves of different plants have different colours, shapes, and sizes; leaves change colour as they grow and eventually fall off; leaves are part of a plant's life cycle; leaves smell different from each other; the life cycle of a plant includes growth, reproduction, and death; observe and record the colour, shape, and size of leaves from different plants; draw and label the parts of a plant, including leaves, flowers, and fruits
learning_objectives: Understand that leaves change color as they grow and eventually fall off
Recognize that leaves are part of a plant's life cycle
Identify that leaves have different colors, shapes, and sizes
Observe and record the characteristics of leaves from different plants
Draw and label the parts of a plant, including leaves, flowers, and fruits

topic: The Sense of Smell in Identifying Fruits
content: The sense of smell can be a powerful tool for identifying fruits, even for those who are visually impaired.
learning_objectives: Understand how the olfactory system allows individuals to detect and differentiate between various scents of fruits.
Recognize that cut fruit may release more aromatic compounds, making it easier to identify by smell compared to whole fruits.
Identify key fruits like mango, pineapple, jackfruit, guava, and jamun based on their distinct smells.

content: Plants are essential for life on Earth as they produce oxygen and are the base of the food chain. The root system of a plant absorbs water and nutrients from the soil, which are vital for the plant's growth. The stem supports the plant, transports nutrients and water between the roots and leaves, and can store food.
learning_objectives: Identify the three main parts of a plant.
Explain why plants are important for the environment.
Describe the function of a plant's stem.

content: Leaves are crucial for plant identification as they vary in shape, size, and texture and can indicate the plant's environment and health.
learning_objectives: Identify plants using leaf characteristics
Understand how environmental factors affect leaves
Recognize the diversity of leaf types among plant species

content: Animals depend on plants for food and shelter. Plants rely on animals for pollination and seed dispersal. The interaction between plants and animals helps maintain the health of the environment.
learning_objectives: Understand the symbiotic relationship between plants and animals.
Identify examples of how animals help plants (e.g., pollination).
Explain why the relationship between plants and animals is important for the environment.

content: Use your fingers, hands, and arms to show how small or big these animals are! Stand on the soil next to a plant, observe its color, pick up soil, and describe its texture and any findings. Smell the soil and discuss why animals live near plants.
learning_objectives: Identify different soil textures through touch and sight
Recognize the presence of organic matter and life forms in soil
Understand the reasons why animals live in the soil

content: Soil is the topmost layer of the Earth's surface, composed of broken-up rocks, organic matter from decomposed plants and animals, and a variety of living organisms.
learning_objectives: Identify the primary components of soil.
Explain how soil biodiversity changes with the seasons.
Describe why soil is considered a living ecosystem.

content: A small hopping insect, a thin plant that curls, a black bird, the grass, and another plant nearby.
learning_objectives: Animals use plants for food, shelter, and nesting.
Plants provide food and shelter to animals.
Symbiotic relationships exist between plants and animals.

content: This learning content is part of the Supratim's initiative of AI learning system designed for students.
learning_objectives: Use hands to cover ears.
Identify bats and leopards.
Recognize their use of tree branches.
Caterpillars eat various plant leaves.
Listen to bird sounds and write down bird names and sounds.
Discuss reasons for not hearing bird sounds.
Note time of day with most bird sounds.
Describe soil color and texture.
Note collection month.
Explain core concept in simple terms.
Point 1
Point 2
Point 3
Explain concept with examples.
Question 1
Question 2
Question 3
Exercise 1
Exercise 2
Mistake 1
Mistake 2
Key point 1
Key point 2
Key point 3

content: The life cycle of a butterfly includes four main stages: egg, caterpillar (larva), chrysalis (pupa), and adult butterfly. The caterpillar's primary role is to eat and grow, storing energy for the transformation. The chrysalis stage is a period of intense change where the caterpillar develops into a butterfly.
learning_objectives: Identify the four stages of a butterfly's life cycle
Explain the role of the caterpillar stage
Describe the transformation process during the chrysalis stage

content: # Chapter: Living in Harmony ## Section: Concept Block 44 ### Core Concept Living in harmony with nature means sharing our homes with various animals, birds, and plants. Some of these living beings are welcomed and cared for, while others are not.
learning_objectives: Understand the concept of living in harmony with nature.
Identify animals and plants that are welcomed in homes and those considered pests.
Recognize the importance of respecting and understanding the role of each creature in the ecosystem.

content: Plants produce oxygen and food, which are vital for animal survival; animals contribute to plant reproduction and pollination, and help in seed dispersal; humans rely on plants and animals for food, medicine, and materials
learning_objectives: Understand the mutualistic relationship between plants and animals
Identify key ways plants benefit animals
Describe how animals assist in plant growth
Recognize the importance of pollinators like bees
Explain the benefits humans derive from plants and animals

content: Animals and plants depend on each other for food and shelter. Animals help plants by dispersing seeds and making the soil fertile through their waste. This helps plants to grow and spread near and far. We also depend on plants and animals for food, shelter, clothing, and companionship.
learning_objectives: Understand the interdependence between animals and plants
Recognize how animals disperse seeds
Identify how animal waste makes soil fertile
List ways humans benefit from animals and plants

content: Ants are attracted to sweet foods like sugar or jaggery, fried foods such as papad or murukku, and boiled or cooked foods like bread, chapati, idli, rice, or ragi.
learning_objectives: Understand why ants prefer sweet foods
Identify types of food ants are attracted to
Recognize how ants benefit from human leftovers

content: Empathy development is crucial for young children, especially those who are reserved. Interactive activities like 'Guess Who I Am?' help children express feelings non-verbally. Sharing feelings with plants and animals fosters a sense of care and support.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of empathy development for reserved children.
Recognize how non-verbal activities aid in expressing feelings for quiet children.
Identify the benefits of sharing feelings with plants and animals.

content: How should we care for plants and animals in our environment?
learning_objectives: Understand that plants and animals depend on us for their survival.
Recognize the importance of protecting habitats and ensuring access to food, water, and shelter.
Acknowledge that our actions significantly impact the well-being of plants and animals.

topic: Migration of Grey Wagtails (Vaalatti kuruvi) and Community Celebrations
content: Grey Wagtails, known as Vaalatti kuruvi, migrate to warmer regions in India during winter and are celebrated in Valparai.
learning_objectives: Understand why Grey Wagtails migrate to warmer regions during winter
Identify ways the local community in Valparai celebrates the arrival of Grey Wagtails
Explain how the name 'Wagtails' reflects the bird's distinctive wagging tail behavior

content: This unit focuses on the essential resources provided by nature, such as water and food, which are crucial for our healthy living.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of consuming healthy meals
Recognize the significance of drinking clean water
Appreciate the need for maintaining personal hygiene
Acknowledge the necessity of regular exercise
Comprehend the importance of conserving Earth's resources
Identify the key concepts of water as a precious gift from nature
Understand the challenges in water conservation
Learn about water conservation measures
Calculate the amount of water used in a household per day
Identify ways to reduce water usage at home
Plan a balanced meal using food from different sources
Demonstrate proper food storage techniques

content: Nutrients from food are vital for our body's health. Cultural knowledge helps us understand the value of different foods. Hygiene and cleanliness are crucial for maintaining health.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of nutrients for body health
Recognize how cultural knowledge aids in making healthy food choices
Identify the significance of hygiene practices in health maintenance

content: Observe the sky for a week and record your observations.
learning_objectives: Identify signs that it might rain
Understand why rain is important for the environment
Learn how to prepare for rain

topic: Understanding Rainwater Behavior and the Water Cycle
content: Observe and describe rain characteristics, collect and analyze rainwater, explain the fate of rainwater, and illustrate the water cycle.
learning_objectives: Describe the characteristics of rain such as intensity, drop size, and direction
Collect and observe rainwater in a vessel
Explain how rainwater interacts with different surfaces
Illustrate the journey of rainwater from the sky to the ground and back
Discuss the role of rainwater in the water cycle
Identify the possible fates of rainwater after it stops raining

content: Rainwater infiltrates the soil and can either flow underground to join bodies of water or evaporate and return to the atmosphere. This process is essential for replenishing groundwater and maintaining the water cycle.
learning_objectives: Understand how rainwater contributes to the water cycle
Describe what happens to rainwater after it infiltrates the soil
Explain why groundwater recharge is important

content: Water is a precious gift from nature that we receive in various ways. It is essential for life and must be cared for responsibly.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of water as a natural resource
Identify different sources of water and methods of storage
Recognize the significance of efficient water usage and pollution prevention
Explain the role of the water cycle in maintaining water sources
List ways to conserve water at home
Describe the impact of water pollution on both the environment and human health
Explain the journey of a water droplet from the ocean to the tap
Identify common mistakes related to water conservation
Recall key points about water conservation and the water cycle

content: Explore the sources and transportation methods of water supply systems in your community.
learning_objectives: Identify the source of water in your home
Describe how water is transported to your home
Explain the importance of clean water in your community

content: List all the activities you can think of for which we need water. How many mugs of water do you need to brush your teeth? How much water do you use to take a shower? Have you ever faced a situation when you did not get any water? What did you do then? Storing water No water today! We have stored two buckets of water. Oh no! The society pipeline burst today. Storing Water Water A Precious Gift When we know that we do not get water all the time then we store it. In some places, people do not get running water through taps and pipes in their homes. They have to bring the water from a river or well. Sometimes it is delivered through water tankers and stored at home. Many years ago, there were no pipes or taps anywhere in the world. So people learned to make containers or vessels to store water.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of water for daily activities
Recognize the practice of storing water in areas without running water
Identify different methods of water storage

content: Vessels have been made from various materials like clay, copper, steel, glass, brass, and aluminium. Different cultures and times have used different types of vessels for water storage. The names of vessels can vary based on the region and language.
learning_objectives: Identify materials used to make vessels for water storage
Explain how the names of vessels for water storage have changed over time
Understand why vessels are important for storing water

content: Water is a vital resource for all life on Earth and is essential for our survival.
learning_objectives: Understand why water is considered a precious resource.
Identify ways to reuse wastewater for other purposes.
Learn methods to conserve water in daily life.

content: Clean drinking water is essential for all living beings and a basic human right.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of clean water for survival and health.
Recognize the role of clean water in supporting biodiversity and ecological balance.
Identify the social implications of community practices related to water provision.

topic: Water Conservation and Local Water Sources
content: Observe and describe the process of rainwater collection and its cleanliness, identify a local water body, write a brief description including its name and any known information, discuss the current use of this water source for drinking, if not used for drinking, explain the reasons and whether it was used in the past
learning_objectives: Water is a valuable resource that must be used wisely
Water conservation is essential
Proper use of water resources is crucial
Understanding the local water sources is important for sustainability
Explain the importance of water conservation with examples
Discuss the impact of water misuse on the environment
Why is water conservation important?
How can we ensure the sustainability of our local water sources?
What are the consequences of water misuse?
Create a plan for collecting and using rainwater in your home
Design a birdbath and list the species that visit it
Using water carelessly
Ignoring the importance of clean water sources

content: Discuss among yourselves and list the activities in your house or outside, due to which water gets polluted or wasted. How can we avoid wastage of water? Think of three solutions with your group and write them in your notebook.
learning_objectives: Identify sources of water pollution and wastage in daily life
Propose methods to conserve water
Understand the importance of fixing leaky faucets and pipes

content: Food is essential for our survival and well-being. It provides us with the necessary nutrients and energy to grow, develop, and maintain our health.
learning_objectives: Understand that food comes in various forms such as fruits, vegetables, grains, and dairy products.
Recognize the importance of a balanced diet to consume a variety of foods for all necessary vitamins and minerals.
Acknowledge that food preferences are influenced by cultural, personal, and health reasons.

content: To become a champion runner, one must not only practice regularly but also maintain a balanced and nutritious diet.
learning_objectives: Regular practice is essential for improving running speed and endurance.
A healthy diet supports physical growth and recovery.
Eating a variety of foods ensures the intake of all necessary nutrients.

content: During the summer, the weather is hot, and we tend to sweat a lot. Sweating causes us to lose water from our bodies, which is why we need to drink more fluids like water, buttermilk, nimbu pani (lime water), aam panna, sugarcane juice, or tender coconut water.
learning_objectives: Hydration is crucial during hot weather to replace the water lost through sweat.
Various summer drinks like buttermilk, nimbu pani, aam panna, sugarcane juice, and tender coconut water are not only refreshing but also help in maintaining the body's water balance.
Foods are grown in fields, and many are consumed in their raw form, while others are cooked.

content: Different kinds of food provide a variety of nutrients that our bodies need to grow and stay healthy. Eating seasonal foods ensures that we consume foods that are fresh and at their peak of nutritional value. Healthy foods are usually fresh, whole, and unprocessed, while unhealthy foods often contain a lot of sugar, salt, and unhealthy fats.
learning_objectives: To understand the importance of a balanced diet
To recognize the benefits of eating seasonal foods
To identify healthy and unhealthy food options

content: # Educational Material: Reducing Food Waste at Home ## Chapter: Reducing Food Waste ### Section: Concept Block 70 ### Core Concept Food waste reduction at home involves planning, preparation, and mindful consumption. By understanding the lifecycle of food items and making conscious choices, families can minimize waste and save resources.
learning_objectives: Plan your meals to avoid buying excess food.
Understand food labels to make informed decisions about food storage and consumption.
Store food correctly to extend its shelf life.

content: Maintaining personal hygiene is essential for keeping our bodies clean and free from diseases. This involves daily routines that ensure our physical and mental well-being.
learning_objectives: Brushing teeth twice a day prevents tooth decay and gum disease.
Washing hands with soap reduces the spread of germs.
Adequate hydration, nutrition, and sleep are foundational to a healthy lifestyle.

content: Washing hands with soap after using the toilet and after coming home from outdoors is essential for maintaining personal hygiene and preventing the spread of germs.
learning_objectives: Handwashing with soap removes dirt, bacteria, and viruses from your hands, reducing the risk of infections.
It is a simple yet effective way to protect yourself and others from illnesses, especially in public spaces.
Developing a habit of handwashing can lead to better overall health and well-being.

content: The modern idea of brushing teeth came from the Indian hygiene tradition of datun using twigs of certain plants including neem, karanj, babool, etc., to clean our teeth.
learning_objectives: Identify natural ingredients used for cleaning and oral hygiene
Compare the effectiveness of natural ingredients to chemical cleaners
List additional natural ingredients for cleaning purposes

content: The mixture needs sunlight to help with the cleaning process, shaking the mixture occasionally helps to mix the ingredients, straining the mixture after two weeks removes any solid residues, leaving a clear liquid cleaner
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of sunlight in activating cleaning agents
Recognize the role of shaking in mixing the ingredients
Appreciate the benefit of straining to remove solid residues

content: Try the following moves: Walk like a duck. Jump like a frog. Run like a cat. Which moves did you enjoy the most? Take a skipping rope and count as you jump. Do this with your friends and see who can jump the maximum number of times. Then, invite a friend to start jumping after you skip 10 times or after you say a short poem as you skip.
learning_objectives: Identify different physical exercises
Engage in friendly competition through games
Practice coordination and rhythm

content: Always follow the rules of the game. Be aware of your surroundings and the people around you. Use appropriate safety gear if required.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of following game rules
Recognize the significance of being aware of surroundings
Identify the need for safety gear in games

topic: Safe Areas for Children to Play
content: Which of the following places is considered a safe area to play? Why or why not? - A construction site: Unsafe - A roof without railings: Unsafe - A park: Safe - A busy road: Unsafe - A schoolground: Safe
learning_objectives: Play in safe areas to avoid injury
A park and schoolground are good places to play
Avoid construction sites and busy roads

content: Explain the concept of dividing a day into 24 slices, each representing one hour.
learning_objectives: Understand the division of a day into 24 slices.
Recognize the importance of daily routines for health.
Identify feelings and their relation to our body's needs.

topic: Dividing a Circle to Represent a 24-Hour Day
content: Now colour these slices differently based on the number of hours you spend doing different activities in one entire day of 24 hours.
learning_objectives: Understand that a circle can be divided into 24 equal parts to represent a full day.
Recognize that each part of the circle represents one hour of the day.
Visualize time spent on different tasks by colouring the parts of the circle.

content: This intuitive drawing exercise is designed to help students understand the relationship between shapes and their proportions.
learning_objectives: Recognize and draw basic shapes
Understand the concept of fractions through shapes
Learn to represent time and national symbols with shapes

content: In this unit, we will explore the many things that we make and use to live comfortably. We will observe these items, learn what they are made of, and discover where they come from. We will delve into the natural resources that provide us with these materials. We will also explore various materials and learn how they are used in different ways. Additionally, we will observe the waste materials around us and learn how to manage them to keep our surroundings clean and healthy.
learning_objectives: Observe and understand the materials and origins of everyday objects
Explore the properties and uses of various materials
Learn about natural resources and their role in providing materials
Identify the natural resources that provide materials for everyday objects
Understand why different materials have diverse properties and uses
Recognize the importance of managing waste materials to keep surroundings clean and healthy

content: Houses are built using natural resources, and people often innovate with new designs and styles. Safety rules are crucial in construction and daily life.
learning_objectives: Understand the use of natural resources in construction
Recognize the importance of innovation in house design
Acknowledge the significance of safety rules in construction and daily activities

content: Draw a picture of your classroom in your notebook. Label the things that you have drawn. Show the children some common metals around you such as iron, copper, aluminium, gold, silver, mercury in a thermometer or alloys such as steel, brass, and bronze. Explain clearly in simple language. Wood is a natural material used to make furniture like tables and chairs. Metals are used to make parts of the door, such as hinges, nails, and latches. Alloys are mixtures of metals that are used to make stronger materials. What materials are commonly used to make furniture? Why are metals used for door hinges and latches? Can you name an alloy and its use? Draw and label the materials used in your classroom. Identify and label the natural and man-made materials in your classroom. Confusing wood with plastic or metal. Not recognizing that alloys are made from combining different metals. Wood is used to make furniture. Metals are used for door parts. Alloys are mixtures of metals for stronger materials.
learning_objectives: Identify and label materials in the classroom
Recognize common metals and their uses
Differentiate between natural and man-made materials
Understand the concept of alloys
Explain the use of wood and metals in furniture and doors

content: Metals are extracted from ores found in rocks and sediments within the Earth and are used in various objects around us, such as in the construction of buildings, manufacturing of vehicles, and creation of electronic devices.
learning_objectives: Understand that metals are typically hard, shiny, malleable, and good conductors of heat and electricity.
Identify common uses of metals in everyday life.
Describe the process of extracting metals from ores.

topic: Properties of Materials in Terms of Light Transmission
content: Materials can be classified based on how well they allow light to pass through them.
learning_objectives: Understand the difference between transparent, opaque, and translucent materials
Identify examples of transparent, opaque, and translucent materials
Explain why we can or cannot see through different types of materials

content: When you look at objects through different transparent materials, the colors you see can change due to the material filtering out certain colors from the light that passes through it.
learning_objectives: Describe how colored transparent materials can change the color of white paper by filtering out specific colors.
Explain how the color of objects can appear different when viewed through colored transparent materials.
Identify the reason behind the change in color perception due to the interaction of light with materials and the eye's perception.

content: Materials like wood, metal, glass, and plastic are used to make everyday objects. Wood is used for furniture and doors, metal for hinges and nails, glass for windowpanes and light bulbs, and plastic for items like pencils.
learning_objectives: Identify materials used in everyday objects
Distinguish between natural and synthetic materials
Understand how material properties determine their uses

topic: Concept Block 88: Sound Produced by Different Materials
content: The material of an object affects the sound it makes when tapped, with wood, metal, plastic, cloth, and glass having distinct sounds due to their physical properties.
learning_objectives: Understand that the material of an object affects the sound it produces when tapped.
Identify common materials (wood, metal, plastic, cloth, glass) and their distinct sounds.
Explain how the flexibility or rigidity of a material influences its sound when tapped.

topic: Classification of Matter Based on Physical State
content: Solids, liquids, and gases are the three main categories of physical states for objects. Solids maintain their shape and volume, liquids have a definite volume but take the shape of their container, and gases have neither a definite shape nor volume.
learning_objectives: Understand the three main categories of physical states for objects: solids, liquids, and gases.
Identify examples of solids, liquids, and gases.
Explain why liquids take the shape of their container.

topic: Classification of Matter and Environmental Impact
content: Objects can be classified as solids, liquids, or gases based on their state of matter. Solids have a definite shape and volume, liquids have a definite volume but no definite shape, and gases have neither a definite shape nor volume. Some objects, like petroleum-based materials, are non-biodegradable and can cause environmental harm.
learning_objectives: Understand the classification of matter into solids, liquids, and gases
Identify the characteristics of solids, liquids, and gases
Recognize examples of non-biodegradable waste

topic: Natural vs Artificial and Material Properties
content: List five natural and five artificial things you encounter in your daily life. Observe the materials around you and classify them as transparent, translucent, or opaque.
learning_objectives: Understand the difference between natural and artificial things
Recognize materials based on their ability to transmit light
Identify common natural and artificial objects
Classify materials as transparent, translucent, or opaque

content: Observe the object's weight and texture, conduct a magnet test, and check for heat conductivity.
learning_objectives: Identify two physical properties to test for metal objects
Explain why a painted object might not be attracted to a magnet
Describe how weight helps in identifying metal objects

content: Pots are made from clay, which is a natural material found in the earth. Clay is shaped and hardened by a process called pottery.
learning_objectives: Identify the primary material used to make pots.
Understand the process of pottery and its stages.
Explain the role of a kiln in pottery making.

content: Pots are used for various purposes such as cooking, storing food, and holding water. Potters create a range of items including pots, plates, bowls, and decorative pieces. The pottery is baked in a kiln to harden the clay and make it durable.
learning_objectives: Understand the uses of pots in daily life.
Recognize the variety of items made by potters.
Appreciate the role of kilns in hardening pottery.

content: Remove impurities, create clay, shape and dry it
learning_objectives: Purify soil by removing impurities
Create clay from purified soil
Shape clay into objects
Understand the importance of drying clay
Identify common mistakes in the process

content: Patterns are repetitive designs that can be found in nature, art, and everyday objects. They can be created by lines, shapes, or colors that repeat in a predictable manner.
learning_objectives: Recognize patterns in nature and human-made objects
Understand how patterns help predict the world around us
Identify common mistakes when recognizing patterns

content: Buildings are constructed using various materials, with bricks being a common choice for their durability and strength. Mud, grass, wood, and cow dung are also used, especially in warm climates, offering environmental benefits and natural insulation.
learning_objectives: List three benefits of using bricks for construction.
Explain why cow dung might be preferred in certain regions.
Describe how natural materials like grass and wood contribute to sustainable building practices.

topic: Materials Used in House Construction and Artistic Inspiration
content: Houses are typically made of various materials such as wood, brick, stone, or concrete. The choice of material depends on the type of house and the local availability of resources. Artists often draw inspiration from their surroundings, personal experiences, or imagination. A kiln is a type of oven used to fire pottery and ceramics, which hardens the clay and makes it durable.
learning_objectives: Identify common materials used in house construction.
Explain how artists get ideas for their artwork.
Describe the purpose of a kiln in pottery making.

content: Waste is created when we use products and perform activities in our daily lives. For example, when we eat food, we often use disposable plates and cups, which become waste after use.
learning_objectives: Understand that waste is generated from daily activities such as eating, drinking, and using packaged products
Identify common types of waste including plastic wrappers, paper, and old batteries
Recognize the importance of proper waste disposal to maintain a clean environment

content: Managing waste is a responsibility that falls on each individual. By learning from communities that effectively handle waste, we can adopt similar practices.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of effective waste management
Identify the consequences of improper waste disposal
Contribute to waste management in the community

content: Reduce waste by avoiding single-use plastics and using reusable bags; participate in cleanlin002dliness drives by donating unwanted items to those in need; learn from communities that manage waste effectively to improve local practices; reducing waste helps conserve resources and protects the environment; throwing waste inappropriately leads to environmental pollution; not participating in or learning from effective waste management practices is a common mistake
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of waste management for a clean and healthy environment
Identify methods to reduce waste in daily life
Describe how to contribute to a cleanliness drive in the community
Explain why learning from effective waste management practices is important
Recognize common mistakes in waste management

content: The core concept revolves around the importance of reducing waste by buying only what we need and reusing items instead of discarding them.
learning_objectives: Purchasing only necessary items and using them until they are no longer functional helps minimize waste.
Reusing items, such as making quilts from old sarees or bags from old clothes, is a sustainable practice that reduces waste.
Creative reuse of materials like newspapers and old cloth for gift wrapping is an eco-friendly way to reduce waste and add a personal touch to gifts.

content: Create an item using waste material that you can give as a gift to someone.
learning_objectives: Reduce waste
Show gratitude
Contribute to cleanliness

topic: Zero Waste Villages and Environmental Management
content: Zero waste villages manage waste without harming the environment by recycling and reusing materials, while other clean cities like Indore and Mysuru participate in the Swachh Bharat Mission.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of zero waste villages in environmental management.
Identify ways to keep the environment clean through waste separation and recycling.
Recognize examples of zero waste villages and clean cities in India.

content: The green dustbin is designated for organic waste such as dried leaves, branches, fruit peels, vegetable scraps, and eggshells.
learning_objectives: Understand that organic waste decomposes and becomes compost, which benefits soil health.
Recognize the importance of separate bins for different types of waste.
Comprehend the process of recycling waste materials into new products.

content: This activity explains the importance of keeping surroundings clean and proper waste disposal.
learning_objectives: Explain the role of wrapping sharp items to protect sanitation workers
List actions to maintain home and classroom cleanliness
Identify tools used for cleaning at school and home
Describe the impact of cleanliness on disease prevention
Discuss the effects of proper waste disposal on the environment
Highlight the benefits of personal hygiene
Pledge to maintain cleanliness and manage waste properly
Practice cleaning and sorting waste
Recognize common mistakes in waste management and personal hygiene
Revise key points about cleanliness, waste disposal, and personal hygiene

content: Waste management is crucial for maintaining a clean and healthy environment. It involves the careful handling, disposal, and recycling of materials that are no longer useful or needed.
learning_objectives: Understand the three R's of waste management: reduce, reuse, and recycle.
Recognize the importance of proper waste management in protecting the environment and conserving resources.
Identify common mistakes in waste management such as throwing recyclable items in the trash and not separating waste into the correct bins.

content: In a birthday party, various types of waste can be generated, such as plastic utensils, paper decorations, and food packaging. To minimize this waste, one can opt for reusable or biodegradable materials and encourage guests to bring their own supplies.
learning_objectives: Identify common waste items at a birthday party.
Choose sustainable alternatives for party supplies.
Encourage guests to participate in waste reduction efforts.
